summary: The text describes cryptographic methods involving letter substitution with vowels, numerical ciphers, and references to a cipher table used for decoding messages, alongside mentions of individuals and locations tied to secret communications.

entities.name: vowel-based cipher
entities.description: Letters are converted into vowels using interposed sounds as per the text's method.
entities.quote: Vowels written openly conceal hidden letters, soft consonants.

entities.name: numerical cipher
entities.description: Numbers are used to represent letters and decode messages via a table or key.
entities.quote: we will explain how all the numbers of letters are changed into vowels

entities.name: Dr. Furrer Wéris
entities.description: A figure who reveals himself as a keyholder for secret communications.

entities.name: Cornelio
entities.description: One of the leaders mentioned to be involved in opening a clanculum for military use.
entities.quote: we will come with Ruralino, CORNELIO, AND OTHER LEADERS

entities.name: caper leaf
entities.description: Used as a symbolic or literal image in the cipher context.
entities.quote: a caper leaf from the mind

entities.name: antiphagum
entities.description: Possibly a plant part or symbolic object referenced in relation to the caper leaf.

entities.name: cipher table
entities.description: A table used for decoding messages by mapping numbers or letters to vowels or other symbols.
entities.quote: we can begin to decipher with one key

entities.name: Pugna te virile
entities.description: A phrase indicating struggle for liberty, possibly symbolic in the cipher context.
entities.quote: PUGNATE VIRILY; FOR I COME so that I may assert liberty in citizenship.

entities.name: Neapolis
entities.description: Where Datum (the work) was published or dated.

entities.name: Laurentius
entities.description: A named individual referenced in the cipher key or table.
